html:
Exclusive to:

Bioluminescence is a passive immobile Mob introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC. It is found in Deep Oceans in groups of 12. It can be considered the aquatic version of Fireflies, and emits light during the night. It will not appear until dusk arrives, and will stay until dawn. It does however still persist during a Full Moon. Once found, it can be caught with Trawl Net or Net while it is visible. Unlike Fireflies, it won't disappear when player character approaches, so they can be used as a permanent protection against Charlie.

Bioluminescence can be used to craft Bottle Lanterns, Buoys, and can be used to refuel Bottle Lanterns, Miner Hats as well as Boat Lanterns (restores 38.5% of its capacity). It does not change location so once the map is generated, its location can be marked on the map by placing any items that have a map icon, such as Backpack, Log Raft, Raft or placing a Buoy nearby.

When dropped on land, Bioluminescence is destroyed, but it can be placed in the water safely.

Trivia

  • Bioluminescence typically refers to the production of light by living organisms. Given the appearance and habitat of the Bioluminescence in-game, it appears to be modelled after Dinoflagellates, which are bioluminescent Plankton that can cause water to glow in a phenomenon known as Red Tide.
  • Before Bioluminescence was made obtainable through trading with the Yaarctopus, it could only be made renewable with a Construction Amulet and a Deconstruction Staff by first using the amulet to craft a Bottle Lantern (or Buoy), and then using the deconstruction staff to get the full materials back. This must be done on water to ensure the bioluminescence isn't destroyed when dropped. With this method, any amount of bioluminescence can be doubled with the right amount of Green Gems.

Bugs

  • Sometimes Bioluminescence does not disappear during the Day.
  • Sometimes Bioluminescence can spawn slightly on land.
  • Bioluminescence has a spawner in the files, but the spawner removes itself after world generation.

–Woodie

–Maxwell
“An intriguing instance of chemiluminescence. And a tiny one at that!”
–Wagstaff

–Wigfrid
“Wooow! Pretty water lights!”
–Webber

–Walani
“Magnifique...”
–Warly
“They light up me life.”
–Woodlegs
“SUCH STUFFS AS DREAMS MADE ON”
–Wilba
“Wet lights”
–Wormwood
“Seaborne glowing stuff!”
–Wheeler

It does not change location so once the map is generated, its location can be marked on the map by placing any items that have a map icon, such as Backpack, Log Raft, Raft or placing a Buoy nearby.

Bioluminescence typically refers to the production of light by living organisms. Given the appearance and habitat of the Bioluminescence in-game, it appears to be modelled after Dinoflagellates, which are bioluminescent Plankton that can cause water to glow in a phenomenon known as Red Tide.
Before Bioluminescence was made obtainable through trading with the Yaarctopus, it could only be made renewable with a Construction Amulet and a Deconstruction Staff by first using the amulet to craft a Bottle Lantern (or Buoy), and then using the deconstruction staff to get the full materials back. This must be done on water to ensure the bioluminescence isn't destroyed when dropped. With this method, any amount of bioluminescence can be doubled with the right amount of Green Gems.

Bioluminescence has a spawner in the files, but the spawner removes itself after world generation.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Phát quang sinh học là một Mob thụ động bất động được giới thiệu trong DLC ​​Shipwrecked. Nó được tìm thấy ở Deep Oceans theo nhóm 12 con. Nó có thể được coi là phiên bản dưới nước của Đom đóm và phát ra ánh sáng vào ban đêm. Nó sẽ không xuất hiện cho đến khi hoàng hôn buông xuống và sẽ tồn tại cho đến bình minh. Tuy nhiên nó vẫn tồn tại trong suốt Trăng tròn. Sau khi được tìm thấy, nó có thể bị bắt bằng Lưới Rà hoặc Lưới khi nó đang hiển thị. Không giống như Đom đóm, nó sẽ không biến mất khi nhân vật của người chơi đến gần, vì vậy chúng có thể được sử dụng như một biện pháp bảo vệ vĩnh viễn chống lại Charlie.

It is found in [[Ocean#Shipwrecked|Deep Oceans]] in groups of 12. It can be considered the aquatic version of [[Fireflies]], and emits light during the [[night]]. It will not appear until dusk arrives, and will stay until dawn. It does however still persist during a [[Full Moon]]. Once found, it can be caught with [[Trawl Net]] or [[Net]] while it is visible. Unlike Fireflies, it won't disappear when player character approaches, so they can be used as a permanent protection against [[Charlie]]. Bioluminescence can be used to craft [[Bottle Lantern]]s, [[Buoy]]s, and can be used to refuel Bottle Lanterns, [[Miner Hat]]s as well as [[Boat Lantern]]s (restores 38.5% of its capacity). It does not change location so once the map is generated, its location can be marked on the [[map]] by placing any items that have a map icon, such as [[Backpack]], [[Log Raft]], [[Raft]] or placing a Buoy nearby. When dropped on land, Bioluminescence is destroyed, but it can be placed in the water safely. =={{pic32|Icon Tools}} Usage== {{Recipe |item1 = Bioluminescence |count1 = 2 |item2 = Empty Bottle |tool = Alchemy Engine |result = Bottle Lantern}} {{Recipe |item1 = Bioluminescence |count1 = 2 |item2 = Empty Bottle |item3 = Bamboo |count3 = 4 |tool = Science Machine |result = Buoy}} == {{pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * [[Wikipedia:Bioluminescence|Bioluminescence]] typically refers to the production of light by living organisms. This must be done on water to ensure the bioluminescence isn't destroyed when dropped. With this method, any amount of bioluminescence can be doubled with the right amount of [[Green Gem]]s. =={{Pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs== * Sometimes Bioluminescence does not disappear during the [[Day]]. * Sometimes Bioluminescence can spawn slightly on land. * Bioluminescence has a spawner in the files, but the spawner removes itself after world generation. {{Mob Dropped Items}} [[fr:Lucioles]] [[Category:Passive Creatures]] [[Category:Light Sources]] [[Category:Fuel]] [[Category:Non-Flammable]] [[Category:Non-Renewable]] [[Category:Resources]]
